mnemonics: NoSQL = Not Only SQL (flexible data models for big data)
CAP Theorem: Choose 2 of 3 - Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance
BASE: Basically Available, Soft state, Eventually consistent (opposite of ACID)
keyPoints: NoSQL databases use flexible schemas rather than the rigid table structure of relational databases
Major types include document, key-value, column-family, and graph databases
Designed for horizontal scaling and distributed data across clusters
commonMistakes: Assuming NoSQL means no schema - schemas are flexible but still exist
Applying relational database design patterns to NoSQL systems
Thinking CAP theorem means you can't have all three properties simultaneously
